response:
Let’s check if \( 22717 \) is prime.  

First, test small primes:  
- Divisible by 3? Sum of digits \( 2+2+7+1+7 = 19 \), not divisible by 3.  
- Divisible by 5? Last digit is 7, so no.  
- Divisible by 7? \( 22717 \div 7 \approx 3245.285...\), not integer.  
- Divisible by 11? Alternating sum: \( 2 - 2 + 7 - 1 + 7 = 13 \), not divisible by 11.  
- Divisible by 13? \( 13 \times 1747 = 22711 \), remainder 6, so no.  
- Divisible by 17? \( 17 \times 1336 = 22712 \), remainder 5, so no.  
- Divisible by 19? \( 19 \times 1195 = 22705 \), remainder 12, so no.  
- Divisible by 23? \( 23 \times 987 = 22701 \), remainder 16, so no.  
- Divisible by 29? \( 29 \times 783 = 22707 \), remainder 10, so no.  
- Divisible by 31? \( 31 \times 732 = 22692 \), remainder 25, so no.  
- Divisible by 37? \( 37 \times 614 = 22718 \), too high; try \( 37 \times 613 = 22681 \), remainder 36, so no.  
- Divisible by 41? \( 41 \times 554 = 22714 \), remainder 3, so no.  
- Divisible by 43? \( 43 \times 528 = 22704 \), remainder 13, so no.  
- Divisible by 47? \( 47 \times 483 = 22701 \), remainder 16, so no.  
- Divisible by 53? \( 53 \times 428 = 22684 \), remainder 33, so no.  
- Divisible by 59? \( 59 \times 385 = 22715 \), remainder 2, so no.  
- Divisible by 61? \( 61 \times 372 = 22692 \), remainder 25, so no.  
- Divisible by 67? \( 67 \times 339 = 22713 \), remainder 4, so no.  
- Divisible by 71? \( 71 \times 320 = 22720 \), too high; \( 71 \times 319 = 22649 \), remainder 68, so no.  
- Divisible by 73? \( 73 \times 311 = 22703 \), remainder 14, so no.  
- Divisible by 79? \( 79 \times 287 = 22673 \), remainder 44, so no.  
- Divisible by 83? \( 83 \times 273 = 22659 \), remainder 58, so no.  
- Divisible by 89? \( 89 \times 255 = 22695 \), remainder 22, so no.  
- Divisible by 97? \( 97 \times 234 = 22698 \), remainder 19, so no.  

We should check up to \( \sqrt{22717} \approx 150.7 \).  

Next, try 101: \( 101 \times 225 = 22725 \), too high; \( 101 \times 224 = 22624 \), remainder 93, so no.  
Try 103: \( 103 \times 220 = 22660 \), remainder 57, so no.  
Try 107: \( 107 \times 212 = 22684 \), remainder 33, so no.  
Try 109: \( 109 \times 208 = 22672 \), remainder 45, so no.  
Try 113: \( 113 \times 201 = 22713 \), remainder 4, so no.  
Try 127: \( 127 \times 178 = 22606 \), remainder 111, so no.  
Try 131: \( 131 \times 173 = 22663 \), remainder 54, so no.  
Try 137: \( 137 \times 165 = 22605 \), remainder 112, so no.  
Try 139: \( 139 \times 163 = 22657 \), remainder 60, so no.  
Try 149: \( 149 \times 152 = 22648 \), remainder 69, so no.  

We’ve checked all primes up to 149, and none divide 22717.  

Thus, \( 22717 \) is prime.  

**Answer:** yes
